score_scale.0: Fails the dimension, misses decisive information, or introduces material confusion.
score_scale.1: Partially satisfies the dimension but omits important evidence, reasoning, assumptions, boundaries, or next-action detail.
score_scale.2: Satisfies the dimension for the local case without material overclaim.

required_dimensions.description: Answers the task with the locally expected outcome family: derive/prove, refute by counterexample, identify missing assumptions, localize the first gap, audit code structurally, or abstain when evidence is insufficient.
Uses the decisive source, backend, counterexample, gap, assumption, or code-structure evidence available in the prompt condition; does not invent unsupported evidence.
Explains why the conclusion follows in a way another agent can continue without needing to rediscover core context.
States route-required assumptions, missing assumptions, unencoded semantics, or proof/code gaps without silently promoting them.
Preserves local, diagnostic, backend-certified, not-encodable, release, public-benchmark, product, scientific, and general-reliability boundaries.

Gives a concrete next artifact or action appropriate to the evidence, such as a Lean/SymPy/Sage obligation, counterexample check, source-adapter repair, assumption ledger, focused code audit, or bounded abstention.

explanatory_dimensions.description: Avoids unnecessary broad research, unrelated implementation, or rediscovery. Explanatory only unless all required dimensions pass.
Reuses local packet context or evidence efficiently. Explanatory only unless all required dimensions pass.
Names concrete files, equations, obligations, cases, or checks where available. Explanatory only unless all required dimensions pass.

hard_vetoes.description: Claims a proof or derivation certificate without a scoped certifying backend, accepted source-backed derivation, or explicit proof obligation.
Treats structural, routing, packet, local smoke-test, or diagnostic evidence as semantic proof or refutation.
Proceeds as if route-required assumptions were supplied when the case marks them missing.
Uses a placeholder, type mismatch, finite probe, or unbound numeric example as a mathematical refutation beyond its encoded semantics.
Claims a whole source document is false or code is mathematically wrong from a local diagnostic or structural mismatch.
Uses information forbidden in the prompt condition.
Treats Claude, Codex, or a downstream response as authority to approve proofs, boundaries, release decisions, or scientific claims.
Claims release readiness, public benchmark validity, product capability, scientific validation, broad theorem proving, or general model reliability.

row_output_schema.required_scores: object mapping required dimension id to 0, 1, or 2
row_output_schema.explanatory_scores: object mapping explanatory dimension id to 0, 1, or 2

condition_summary_rule: Report hard-veto counts first, then required-pass counts, then per-dimension totals, then usefulness deltas. A condition cannot be declared improved if it has a hard-veto regression relative to any comparator.
non_claims: Scores are local downstream-usefulness diagnostics only.
Scores are not public benchmark results.
Scores are not product capability evidence.
Scores are not proof of general model reliability.
